es.meaning: I just arrived — recency via the acabar de + infinitive periphrasis.

it.meaning: I just arrived — recency via appena + passato prossimo.

bridge.note: Structurally unrelated solutions to the same need: Spanish builds a periphrasis (acabar de + infinitive), Italian just drops appena into a normal past tense. Doubles as auxiliary drill — sono arrivato, but ho finito.

interference.es_to_it: Two layers: there is no acabar-periphrasis to calque ('ho appena finito di arrivare' is nonsense — say sono appena arrivato), and arrivare takes essere, so 'ho appena arrivato' fails twice.
interference.it_to_es: appena tempts 'apenas llegué' — but Spanish apenas means barely / as soon as, not 'just now'. Recency needs the periphrasis: acabo de llegar.

review.notes: Submitted by Joe 2026-07-14. appena/apenas false friend flagged in it→es; three traps in one card. Masculine 'arrivato' in headword — reviewer to confirm we surface agreement (arrivata) in the rendered card.

cue: How do you say you've just done something?
